prompt:
Refactor tools.go and agent.go by splitting them into per-service files. This reduces merge conflicts when multiple agents work on different services in parallel.

## Current state
- internal/agent/tools.go (439 lines) — contains ALL tool definitions and input structs for every service
- internal/agent/agent.go (605 lines) — contains the monolithic dispatchTool() switch with cases for every service

## Target structure

### Tool definitions — split into per-service files:
- internal/agent/tools.go — keep only: toolDef struct, generateSchema(), allToolDefs(), destructiveTools map, IsDestructive()
- internal/agent/tools_sonarr.go — sonarrToolDefs() + all sonarr input structs (searchSeriesInput, addSeriesInput, etc.)
- internal/agent/tools_radarr.go — radarrToolDefs() + all radarr input structs
- internal/agent/tools_prowlarr.go — prowlarrToolDefs() + all prowlarr input structs
- internal/agent/tools_overseerr.go — overseerrToolDefs() + all overseerr input structs
- internal/agent/tools_notebook.go — notebookToolDefs() + notebook input structs

### Dispatch — split into per-service methods:
- internal/agent/agent.go — keep dispatchTool() but have it delegate to per-service methods
- internal/agent/dispatch_sonarr.go — func (a *Agent) dispatchSonarr(ctx, name, rawInput) (string, bool)
- internal/agent/dispatch_radarr.go — func (a *Agent) dispatchRadarr(ctx, name, rawInput) (string, bool)
- internal/agent/dispatch_prowlarr.go — func (a *Agent) dispatchProwlarr(ctx, name, rawInput) (string, bool)
- internal/agent/dispatch_overseerr.go — func (a *Agent) dispatchOverseerr(ctx, name, rawInput) (string, bool)
- internal/agent/dispatch_notebook.go — func (a *Agent) dispatchNotebook(ctx, name, rawInput) (string, bool)

### How dispatchTool() should delegate:
The current switch already groups cases by service. Replace the service-specific cases with delegation:

```go
func (a *Agent) dispatchTool(ctx context.Context, name string, rawInput json.RawMessage) (string, bool) {
    // Try each service dispatcher. Each returns ("", false) if the tool doesn't belong to it.
    if result, handled := a.dispatchSonarr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
        return result, true
    }
    if result, handled := a.dispatchRadarr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
        return result, true
    }
    if result, handled := a.dispatchProwlarr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
        return result, true
    }
    if result, handled := a.dispatchOverseerr(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
        return result, true
    }
    if result, handled := a.dispatchNotebook(ctx, name, rawInput); handled {
        return result, true
    }
    return jsonError("unknown tool: " + name), true
}
```

Each per-service dispatcher has its own switch and returns ("", false) for the default case.

## Important constraints
- All files stay in package agent — this is just file splitting, no new packages
- No behavioral changes — identical functionality, just reorganized
- The destructiveTools map stays in tools.go (it's cross-cutting)
- allToolDefs() stays in tools.go and calls the per-service funcs (already does this)
- Run 'go build ./...' and 'go test ./...' to verify nothing breaks
- Existing tests should pass without modification
